content: I am Rajiv Desai, CFO of VedaCorp since 2019. I admit to preparing some financial documents that were later found to be inaccurate, but I did so under direct orders from Arjun Mehta. I did not initiate the fraud. The shell companies were created by Priya Mehta — she told me they were 'strategic partners' for international contracts. I signed the forged board minutes because I was told Mr. Ravi Nair had authorized them via email — I did not verify his physical presence. The ₹5 crore transfer to Sahara Ventures was a repayment from a client named Sahara Ventures, which I believed was legitimate. I later learned from Dr. Kulkarni that she had been approached by unknown persons threatening her family, which is why she retracted her initial statement. I was never bribed. I acted out of fear for my job and family. I swear this is the truth under Section 164 CrPC.
